revision.comment: 新しいリダイレクト作成（前方後円墳のカウント）。

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 双六古墳 |画像 = [[File:Souroku Kofun, zenkei.jpg|250px]]
墳丘全景（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |所属 = |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]勝本町立石東触81 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|47|54.31|N|129|42|13.35|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline|name=双六古墳}} |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長91m
高さ10.6m |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]]（2室） |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半頃 |被葬者 = |出土品 = 金製品8点・銀製品54点・金銅製品58点など多数 |史跡 = |有形文化財 = 出土品（国の[[重要文化財]]） |特記事項 = 長崎県第1位の規模 }} '''双六古墳'''（そうろくこふん）は、壱岐市勝本町立石東触にある古墳。形状は前方後円墳。 壱岐島、ひいては長崎県最大の規模の古墳である[http://www.ikikankou.com/wp/?p=3811 双六古墳]（壱岐市観光連盟「壱岐観光ナビ」）。。[[6世紀]]後半頃の築造と推定される。 [[1981年]]（昭和56年）に松永泰彦による石室測量が、[[1992年]]（平成4年）に長崎県教育委員会による墳丘・石室の実測調査が、[[1997年]]-[[2000年]]（平成9-12年）に旧勝本町による本格的な発掘調査がなされている{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 === 規模 === 墳丘は前方後円形で、2段築成である{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。その規模は次の通り{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 :* 墳丘長：91メートル :* 後円部：直径43メートル、高さ10.6メートル :* くびれ部：幅24メートル :* 前方部：長さ55メートル、幅36メートル、高さ5メートル 対馬塚古墳同様、墳丘の前方部は後円部に比して長く、後円部はお椀を伏せたような急斜面をなす特徴を有している{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/239 双六古墳]（長崎県教育庁学芸文化課「長崎県の遺跡大辞典」）。。 === 埋葬施設 === 埋葬施設は複室構造の横穴式石室で、玄室・前室の2室と羨道で構成され、南西方に開口する{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。江戸時代にはすでに石室は開口し、『甲子夜話』『壱岐名勝図誌』に記述がある{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 :* 石室全長：11メートル :* 玄室：長さ3.5メートル、幅2.58メートル、高さ4.28メートル :* 前室：長さ6.28メートル、幅1.8メートル、高さ1.68メートル :* 羨道：長さ1.28メートル、幅1.28メートル、高さ1.7メートル 玄室には朱塗痕があるほか、玄室床面には全面に凝灰岩製の敷石が敷かれたと見られる{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。また、石室壁に船形の線刻画も見られる。 === 出土品 === 出土品としては、玄室からは須恵器・土師器・鉄製品・ガラス玉・二彩片・金糸などが、前室からは金銅製圭頭大刀把頭・把元・八窓鐔・雲珠・杏葉・金銅製冠・須恵器・土師器・ガラス玉などが、羨道からは金銅製鈴・鉄鏃・銀象嵌鐔・馬具・土師器・短刀などが、開口部からは新羅土器が見つかっている{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。これらのうち、馬具装飾には高い技術水準が指摘されるほか、大陸産・新羅産の遺物は当時の海外交渉を伝えるものとして重要視される。これらの遺物は「長崎県双六古墳出土品」として国の重要文化財に指定されている。 File:Souroku Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|開口部 File:Souroku Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|石室内部 {{Coord|33|47|54.31|N|129|42|13.35|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=title|name=双六古墳}} {{デフォルトソート:そうろくこふん}} [[Category:長崎県の古墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:装飾古墳]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 双六古墳 |画像 = [[File:Souroku Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション = 墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = [[壱岐古墳群]] |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]勝本町立石東触81 |緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 47|緯度秒 = 54.30 |経度度 = 129|経度分 = 42|経度秒 = 13.35 |ISO = JP-42 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長91m
高さ10.6m |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]]（2室） |出土品 = 金製品・銀製品・金銅製品ほか副葬品多数 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「壱岐古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = 出土品（国の[[重要文化財]]） |特記事項 = 長崎県第1位の規模 |地図 = Japan Nagasaki |アイコン = 前方後円墳-南南東 |ラベル = 双六古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''双六古墳'''（そうろくこふん）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]勝本町立石東触にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[壱岐古墳群]]（国の[[史跡]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。出土品は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 壱岐島、ひいては長崎県最大の規模の古墳で[http://www.ikikankou.com/wp/?p=3811 双六古墳]（壱岐市観光連盟「壱岐観光ナビ」）。、[[6世紀]]後半（[[古墳時代]]後期）頃の築造と推定される。 == 概要 == 壱岐島中央部の丘陵上に築造された古墳である。[[1981年]]（昭和56年）に松永泰彦による石室測量が、[[1992年]]（平成4年）に長崎県教育委員会による墳丘・石室の実測調査が、[[1997年]]-[[2000年]]（平成9-12年）に旧勝本町による本格的な発掘調査がなされている{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘は前方後円形で、2段築成である{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。規模は次の通り{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 墳丘長：91メートル * 後円部：直径43メートル、高さ10.6メートル * くびれ部：幅24メートル * 前方部：長さ55メートル、幅36メートル、高さ5メートル 対馬塚古墳同様、墳丘の前方部は後円部に比して長く、後円部はお椀を伏せたような急斜面をなす特徴を有している{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/239 双六古墳]（長崎県教育庁学芸文化課「長崎県の遺跡大辞典」）。。 == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設は複室構造の横穴式石室で、玄室・前室の2室と羨道で構成され、南西方に開口する{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。江戸時代にはすでに石室は開口し、『甲子夜話』『壱岐名勝図誌』に記述がある{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 石室全長：11メートル * 玄室：長さ3.5メートル、幅2.58メートル、高さ4.28メートル * 前室：長さ6.28メートル、幅1.8メートル、高さ1.68メートル * 羨道：長さ1.28メートル、幅1.28メートル、高さ1.7メートル 玄室には朱塗痕があるほか、玄室床面には全面に凝灰岩製の敷石が敷かれたと見られる{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。また、石室壁に船形の線刻画も見られる。 == 出土品 == 出土品としては、玄室からは須恵器・土師器・鉄製品・ガラス玉・二彩片・金糸などが、前室からは金銅製圭頭大刀把頭・把元・八窓鐔・雲珠・杏葉・金銅製冠・須恵器・土師器・ガラス玉などが、羨道からは金銅製鈴・鉄鏃・銀象嵌鐔・馬具・土師器・短刀などが、開口部からは新羅土器が見つかっている{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。これらのうち、馬具装飾には高い技術水準が指摘されるほか、大陸産・新羅産の遺物は当時の海外交渉を伝えるものとして重要視される。これらの遺物は「長崎県双六古墳出土品」として国の重要文化財に指定されている。 File:Souroku Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|開口部 File:Souroku Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|石室内部 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 長崎県双六古墳出土品（考古資料） - 明細は以下。2008年（平成20年）7月10日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|00011284|長崎県双六古墳出土品}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/481 長崎県双六古墳出土品]（長崎県ホームページ）。。 ** 金製品 8点 ** 銀製品 54点 ** 金銅製品 58点 ** 青銅製品 14点 ** 鉄製品 102点 ** ガラス製品 93点 ** 石製品 10点 ** 琥珀玉 1点 ** 陶器・土器 72点 == 関連施設 == * [[壱岐市立一支国博物館]]（壱岐市芦辺町深江鶴亀触） - 双六古墳の出土品を保管。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板 * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2017|chapter=|title=壱岐市の文化財|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|壱岐市の文化財|2017}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2001|chapter=双六古墳|title=長崎県の地名|volume=[[日本歴史地名大系]]43|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490433|ref={{Harvid|双六古墳（平凡社）|2001}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=田川肇|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/14748 |title=県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書 -県内古墳の墳丘・石室の資料化に伴う報告書-|volume=長崎県文化財調査報告書第106集|publisher=長崎県教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=|title=双六古墳|volume=壱岐市文化財調査報告書第7集|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=|title=壱岐の島の古墳群 -現状調査-|volume=壱岐市文化財調査報告書第20集|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Souroku Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 壱岐古墳群] - 長崎県ホームページ「長崎県の文化財」 {{デフォルトソート:そうろくこふん}} [[Category:壱岐古墳群]] [[Category:長崎県の古墳]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:長崎県の重要文化財]] [[Category:長崎県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:装飾古墳]]

高さ10.6m |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]]（2室） |出土品 = 金製品・銀製品・金銅製品ほか副葬品多数 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「壱岐古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = 出土品（国の[[重要文化財]]） |特記事項 = 長崎県第1位の規模 |地図 = Japan Nagasaki |アイコン = 前方後円墳-南南東 |ラベル = 双六古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''双六古墳'''（そうろくこふん）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]勝本町立石東触にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[壱岐古墳群]]（国の[[史跡]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。出土品は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 壱岐島、ひいては長崎県最大の規模の古墳で[http://www.ikikankou.com/wp/?p=3811 双六古墳]（壱岐市観光連盟「壱岐観光ナビ」）。、[[6世紀]]後半（[[古墳時代]]後期）頃の築造と推定される。 == 概要 == 壱岐島中央部の丘陵上に築造された古墳である。[[1981年]]（昭和56年）に松永泰彦による石室測量が、[[1992年]]（平成4年）に長崎県教育委員会による墳丘・石室の実測調査が、[[1997年]]-[[2000年]]（平成9-12年）に旧勝本町による本格的な発掘調査がなされている{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘は前方後円形で、2段築成である{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。規模は次の通り{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 墳丘長：91メートル * 後円部：直径43メートル、高さ10.6メートル * くびれ部：幅24メートル * 前方部：長さ55メートル、幅36メートル、高さ5メートル 対馬塚古墳同様、墳丘の前方部は後円部に比して長く、後円部はお椀を伏せたような急斜面をなす特徴を有している{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/239 双六古墳]（長崎県教育庁学芸文化課「長崎県の遺跡大辞典」）。。 == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設は複室構造の横穴式石室で、玄室・前室の2室と羨道で構成され、南西方に開口する{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。江戸時代にはすでに石室は開口し、『甲子夜話』『壱岐名勝図誌』に記述がある{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 石室全長：11メートル * 玄室：長さ3.5メートル、幅2.58メートル、高さ4.28メートル * 前室：長さ6.28メートル、幅1.8メートル、高さ1.68メートル * 羨道：長さ1.28メートル、幅1.28メートル、高さ1.7メートル 玄室には朱塗痕があるほか、玄室床面には全面に凝灰岩製の敷石が敷かれたと見られる{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。また、石室壁に船形の線刻画も見られる。 == 出土品 == 出土品としては、玄室からは須恵器・土師器・鉄製品・ガラス玉・二彩片・金糸などが、前室からは金銅製圭頭大刀把頭・把元・八窓鐔・雲珠・杏葉・金銅製冠・須恵器・土師器・ガラス玉などが、羨道からは金銅製鈴・鉄鏃・銀象嵌鐔・馬具・土師器・短刀などが、開口部からは新羅土器が見つかっている{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。これらのうち、馬具装飾には高い技術水準が指摘されるほか、大陸産・新羅産の遺物は当時の海外交渉を伝えるものとして重要視される。これらの遺物は「長崎県双六古墳出土品」として国の重要文化財に指定されている。 File:Souroku Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|開口部 File:Souroku Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|石室内部 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 長崎県双六古墳出土品（考古資料） - 明細は以下。2008年（平成20年）7月10日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|00011284|長崎県双六古墳出土品}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/481 長崎県双六古墳出土品]（長崎県ホームページ）。。 ** 金製品 8点 ** 銀製品 54点 ** 金銅製品 58点 ** 青銅製品 14点 ** 鉄製品 102点 ** ガラス製品 93点 ** 石製品 10点 ** 琥珀玉 1点 ** 陶器・土器 72点 == 関連施設 == * [[壱岐市立一支国博物館]]（壱岐市芦辺町深江鶴亀触） - 双六古墳の出土品を保管。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板 * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2017|chapter=|title=壱岐市の文化財|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|壱岐市の文化財|2017}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2001|chapter=双六古墳|title=長崎県の地名|volume=[[日本歴史地名大系]]43|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490433|ref={{Harvid|双六古墳（平凡社）|2001}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=田川肇|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/14748 |title=県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書 -県内古墳の墳丘・石室の資料化に伴う報告書-|volume=長崎県文化財調査報告書第106集|publisher=長崎県教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=|title=双六古墳|volume=壱岐市文化財調査報告書第7集|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=|title=壱岐の島の古墳群 -現状調査-|volume=壱岐市文化財調査報告書第20集|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Soroku Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 壱岐古墳群] - 長崎県ホームページ「長崎県の文化財」 {{デフォルトソート:そうろくこふん}} [[Category:壱岐古墳群]] [[Category:長崎県の古墳]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:長崎県の重要文化財]] [[Category:長崎県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:装飾古墳]]

[[Category:双六古墳]][[ja:双六古墳]]

'''Sōroku Kofun'''
'''Sōroku Kofun''' (双六古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') located in Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is part of the Iki Kofun Cluster, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan. Artifacts excavated from the site are designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.
This kofun is the largest in Iki Island and Nagasaki Prefecture, estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century during the Kofun period.
=== Overview === The kofun is situated on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Stone chamber surveys were conducted in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, followed by mound and chamber surveys in 1992 by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education. Extensive excavations were carried out by the former Katsumoto Town from 1997 to 2000.
=== Mound === The mound is keyhole-shaped with a two-tiered structure. The dimensions are as follows:
* '''Total Length:''' 91 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' 43 meters in diameter, 10.6 meters in height * '''Constriction Width:''' 24 meters * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' 55 meters long, 36 meters wide, 5 meters high
Similar to the Tsushimazuka Kofun, the front rectangular mound is notably longer compared to the rear circular mound, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl.
=== Burial Chamber === The burial facility consists of a horizontally-arranged stone chamber with a two-room structure: the main chamber (''gen-shitsu''), an antechamber (''zen-shitsu''), and a passage (''ensen-dō''), opening to the southwest. Records from the Edo period, such as ''Kōshi Yawa'' and ''Iki Meishō Zushi'', mention the already-opened chamber.
'''Chamber Dimensions:'''
* '''Total Length:''' 11 meters * '''Main Chamber:''' 3.5 meters long, 2.58 meters wide, 4.28 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 6.28 meters long, 1.8 meters wide, 1.68 meters high * '''Passage:''' 1.28 meters long, 1.28 meters wide, 1.7 meters high
Traces of red pigment are visible in the main chamber, and tuff paving stones cover the floor. Additionally, line engravings of boat shapes are found on the chamber walls.
=== Excavated Artifacts === Artifacts from the site include:

'''Sōroku Kofun'''
'''Sōroku Kofun''' (双六古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') located in Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is part of the Iki Kofun Cluster, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan. Artifacts excavated from the site are designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.
This kofun is the largest in Iki Island and Nagasaki Prefecture, estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century during the Kofun period.
=== Overview === The kofun is situated on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Stone chamber surveys were conducted in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, followed by mound and chamber surveys in 1992 by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education. Extensive excavations were carried out by the former Katsumoto Town from 1997 to 2000.
=== Mound === The mound is keyhole-shaped with a two-tiered structure. The dimensions are as follows:
* '''Total Length:''' 91 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' 43 meters in diameter, 10.6 meters in height * '''Constriction Width:''' 24 meters * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' 55 meters long, 36 meters wide, 5 meters high
Similar to the Tsushimazuka Kofun, the front rectangular mound is notably longer compared to the rear circular mound, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl.
=== Burial Chamber === The burial facility consists of a horizontally-arranged stone chamber with a two-room structure: the main chamber (''gen-shitsu''), an antechamber (''zen-shitsu''), and a passage (''ensen-dō''), opening to the southwest. Records from the Edo period, such as ''Kōshi Yawa'' and ''Iki Meishō Zushi'', mention the already-opened chamber.
'''Chamber Dimensions:'''
* '''Total Length:''' 11 meters * '''Main Chamber:''' 3.5 meters long, 2.58 meters wide, 4.28 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 6.28 meters long, 1.8 meters wide, 1.68 meters high * '''Passage:''' 1.28 meters long, 1.28 meters wide, 1.7 meters high
Traces of red pigment are visible in the main chamber, and tuff paving stones cover the floor. Additionally, line engravings of boat shapes are found on the chamber walls.
=== Excavated Artifacts === Artifacts from the site include:
* '''Main Chamber:''' Sue ware, Haji ware, iron objects, glass beads, polychrome fragments, gold threads * '''Antechamber:''' Gilt-bronze sword hilts, pommel decorations, eight-window guards, ''unju'' ornaments, apricot-leaf pendants, gilt-bronze crowns, Sue ware, Haji ware, glass beads * '''Passage:''' Gilt-bronze bells, iron arrowheads, silver-inlaid guards, horse gear, Haji ware, daggers * '''Entrance Area:''' Silla pottery
These artifacts, particularly the elaborately crafted horse gear and Silla imports, highlight advanced craftsmanship and international interactions during the period. They are collectively designated as ''Important Cultural Properties of Japan'' under the title "Excavated Artifacts from Sōroku Kofun."
=== Gallery ===
* Entrance of the stone chamber * Interior of the stone chamber
=== Cultural Designations ===
* '''Important Cultural Property (National Designation):''' ** ''Excavated Artifacts from Sōroku Kofun'' (designated in 2008), including: *** 8 gold items *** 54 silver items *** 58 gilt-bronze items *** 14 bronze items *** 102 iron items *** 93 glass items *** 10 stone items *** 1 amber bead *** 72 ceramic/earthenware items

'''Sōroku Kofun'''
'''Sōroku Kofun''' (双六古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') located in Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is part of the Iki Kofun Cluster, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan. Artifacts excavated from the site are designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.
This kofun is the largest in Iki Island and Nagasaki Prefecture, estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century during the Kofun period.
=== Overview === The kofun is situated on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Stone chamber surveys were conducted in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, followed by mound and chamber surveys in 1992 by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education. Extensive excavations were carried out by the former Katsumoto Town from 1997 to 2000.
=== Mound === The mound is keyhole-shaped with a two-tiered structure. The dimensions are as follows:
* '''Total Length:''' 91 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' 43 meters in diameter, 10.6 meters in height * '''Constriction Width:''' 24 meters * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' 55 meters long, 36 meters wide, 5 meters high
Similar to the Tsushimazuka Kofun, the front rectangular mound is notably longer compared to the rear circular mound, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl.
=== Burial Chamber === The burial facility consists of a horizontally-arranged stone chamber with a two-room structure: the main chamber (''gen-shitsu''), an antechamber (''zen-shitsu''), and a passage (''ensen-dō''), opening to the southwest. Records from the Edo period, such as ''Kōshi Yawa'' and ''Iki Meishō Zushi'', mention the already-opened chamber.
'''Chamber Dimensions:'''
* '''Total Length:''' 11 meters * '''Main Chamber:''' 3.5 meters long, 2.58 meters wide, 4.28 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 6.28 meters long, 1.8 meters wide, 1.68 meters high * '''Passage:''' 1.28 meters long, 1.28 meters wide, 1.7 meters high
Traces of red pigment are visible in the main chamber, and tuff paving stones cover the floor. Additionally, line engravings of boat shapes are found on the chamber walls.
=== Excavated Artifacts === Artifacts from the site include:
* '''Main Chamber:''' Sue ware, Haji ware, iron objects, glass beads, polychrome fragments, gold threads * '''Antechamber:''' Gilt-bronze sword hilts, pommel decorations, eight-window guards, ''unju'' ornaments, apricot-leaf pendants, gilt-bronze crowns, Sue ware, Haji ware, glass beads * '''Passage:''' Gilt-bronze bells, iron arrowheads, silver-inlaid guards, horse gear, Haji ware, daggers * '''Entrance Area:''' Silla pottery
These artifacts, particularly the elaborately crafted horse gear and Silla imports, highlight advanced craftsmanship and international interactions during the period. They are collectively designated as ''Important Cultural Properties of Japan'' under the title "Excavated Artifacts from Sōroku Kofun."
=== Gallery ===
* Entrance of the stone chamber * Interior of the stone chamber
=== Cultural Designations ===
* '''Important Cultural Property (National Designation):''' ** ''Excavated Artifacts from Sōroku Kofun'' (designated in 2008), including: *** 8 gold items *** 54 silver items *** 58 gilt-bronze items *** 14 bronze items *** 102 iron items *** 93 glass items *** 10 stone items *** 1 amber bead *** 72 ceramic/earthenware items
=== Related Facilities ===
* '''Iki City Ikikoku Museum''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) – houses artifacts excavated from Sōroku Kofun.
=== External Links ===
* Iki Kofun Cluster - Nagasaki Prefectural Website

'''Sōroku Kofun'''
'''Sōroku Kofun''' (双六古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') located in Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is part of the Iki Kofun Cluster, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan. Artifacts excavated from the site are designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.
This kofun is the largest in Iki Island and Nagasaki Prefecture, estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century during the Kofun period.
== Overview == The kofun is situated on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Stone chamber surveys were conducted in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, followed by mound and chamber surveys in 1992 by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education. Extensive excavations were carried out by the former Katsumoto Town from 1997 to 2000.
=== Mound === The mound is keyhole-shaped with a two-tiered structure. The dimensions are as follows:
* '''Total Length:''' 91 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' 43 meters in diameter, 10.6 meters in height * '''Constriction Width:''' 24 meters * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' 55 meters long, 36 meters wide, 5 meters high
Similar to the Tsushimazuka Kofun, the front rectangular mound is notably longer compared to the rear circular mound, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl.
=== Burial Chamber === The burial facility consists of a horizontally-arranged stone chamber with a two-room structure: the main chamber (''gen-shitsu''), an antechamber (''zen-shitsu''), and a passage (''ensen-dō''), opening to the southwest. Records from the Edo period, such as ''Kōshi Yawa'' and ''Iki Meishō Zushi'', mention the already-opened chamber.
'''Chamber Dimensions:'''
* '''Total Length:''' 11 meters * '''Main Chamber:''' 3.5 meters long, 2.58 meters wide, 4.28 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 6.28 meters long, 1.8 meters wide, 1.68 meters high * '''Passage:''' 1.28 meters long, 1.28 meters wide, 1.7 meters high
Traces of red pigment are visible in the main chamber, and tuff paving stones cover the floor. Additionally, line engravings of boat shapes are found on the chamber walls.
=== Excavated Artifacts === Artifacts from the site include:
* '''Main Chamber:''' Sue pottery, Haji pottery, iron objects, glass beads, polychrome fragments, gold threads * '''Antechamber:''' Gilt-bronze sword hilts, pommel decorations, eight-window guards, ''unju'' ornaments, apricot-leaf pendants, gilt-bronze crowns, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, glass beads * '''Passage:''' Gilt-bronze bells, iron arrowheads, silver-inlaid guards, horse gear, Haji pottery, daggers * '''Entrance Area:''' Silla pottery
These artifacts, particularly the elaborately crafted horse gear and Silla imports, highlight advanced craftsmanship and international interactions during the period. They are collectively designated as ''Important Cultural Properties of Japan'' under the title "Excavated Artifacts from Sōroku Kofun."
=== Gallery ===
* Entrance of the stone chamber * Interior of the stone chamber
=== Cultural Designations ===
* '''Important Cultural Property (National Designation):''' ** ''Excavated Artifacts from Sōroku Kofun'' (designated in 2008), including: *** 8 gold items *** 54 silver items *** 58 gilt-bronze items *** 14 bronze items *** 102 iron items *** 93 glass items *** 10 stone items *** 1 amber bead *** 72 ceramic/earthenware items
=== Related Facilities ===
* '''Iki City Ikikoku Museum''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) – houses artifacts excavated from Sōroku Kofun.
=== External Links ===
* Iki Kofun Cluster - Nagasaki Prefectural Website

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Sōroku Kofun'''
'''Sōroku Kofun''' (双六古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') located in Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is part of the Iki Kofun Cluster, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan. Artifacts excavated from the site are designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.
This kofun is the largest in Iki Island and Nagasaki Prefecture, estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century during the Kofun period.
== Overview == The kofun is situated on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Stone chamber surveys were conducted in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, followed by mound and chamber surveys in 1992 by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education. Extensive excavations were carried out by the former Katsumoto Town from 1997 to 2000.
=== Mound === The mound is keyhole-shaped with a two-tiered structure. The dimensions are as follows:
* '''Total Length:''' 91 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' 43 meters in diameter, 10.6 meters in height * '''Constriction Width:''' 24 meters * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' 55 meters long, 36 meters wide, 5 meters high
Similar to the Tsushimazuka Kofun, the front rectangular mound is notably longer compared to the rear circular mound, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl.
=== Burial Chamber === The burial facility consists of a horizontally-arranged stone chamber with a two-room structure: the main chamber (''gen-shitsu''), an antechamber (''zen-shitsu''), and a passage (''ensen-dō''), opening to the southwest. Records from the Edo period, such as ''Kōshi Yawa'' and ''Iki Meishō Zushi'', mention the already-opened chamber.
'''Chamber Dimensions:'''
* '''Total Length:''' 11 meters * '''Main Chamber:''' 3.5 meters long, 2.58 meters wide, 4.28 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 6.28 meters long, 1.8 meters wide, 1.68 meters high * '''Passage:''' 1.28 meters long, 1.28 meters wide, 1.7 meters high
Traces of red pigment are visible in the main chamber, and tuff paving stones cover the floor. Additionally, line engravings of boat shapes are found on the chamber walls.
=== Excavated Artifacts === Artifacts from the site include:
* '''Main Chamber:''' Sue pottery, Haji pottery, iron objects, glass beads, polychrome fragments, gold threads * '''Antechamber:''' Gilt-bronze sword hilts, pommel decorations, eight-window guards, ''unju'' ornaments, apricot-leaf pendants, gilt-bronze crowns, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, glass beads * '''Passage:''' Gilt-bronze bells, iron arrowheads, silver-inlaid guards, horse gear, Haji pottery, daggers * '''Entrance Area:''' Silla pottery
These artifacts, particularly the elaborately crafted horse gear and Silla imports, highlight advanced craftsmanship and international interactions during the period. They are collectively designated as ''Important Cultural Properties of Japan'' under the title "Excavated Artifacts from Sōroku Kofun."
=== Gallery ===
* Entrance of the stone chamber * Interior of the stone chamber
=== Cultural Designations ===
* '''Important Cultural Property (National Designation):''' ** ''Excavated Artifacts from Sōroku Kofun'' (designated in 2008), including: *** 8 gold items *** 54 silver items *** 58 gilt-bronze items *** 14 bronze items *** 102 iron items *** 93 glass items *** 10 stone items *** 1 amber bead *** 72 ceramic/earthenware items
=== Related Facilities ===
* '''Iki City Ikikoku Museum''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) – houses artifacts excavated from Sōroku Kofun.
=== External Links ===
* Iki Kofun Cluster - Nagasaki Prefectural Website

* Iki Kofun Cluster * Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture * History of Iki City * Keyhole-shaped Kofun * Important Cultural Properties of Japan (Nagasaki Prefecture) * National Historic Sites of Japan (Nagasaki Prefecture) * Decorated Kofun

Bur ...== 4 KB (487 words) - 06:01, 19 January 2025 == Iki Kofun Group * {{ill|Sugoroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|12=draft}} - Keyhole-shaped mound (total length: 91 meters; the largest in Iki Islan ...{{ill|Tsushima-tsuka Kofun|ja|対馬塚古墳|12=draft}}, followed by {{ill|Sugoroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|12=draft}}, and later transitioned to circular mounds by the end of the century. '''Sōroku Kofun'''
'''Sōroku Kofun''' (双六古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') located in Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is part of the Iki Kofun Cluster, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan. Artifacts excavated from the site are designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.
This kofun is the largest in Iki Island and Nagasaki Prefecture, estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century during the Kofun period.
== Overview == The kofun is situated on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Stone chamber surveys were conducted in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, followed by mound and chamber surveys in 1992 by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education. Extensive excavations were carried out by the former Katsumoto Town from 1997 to 2000.
=== Mound === The mound is keyhole-shaped with a two-tiered structure. The dimensions are as follows:
* '''Total Length:''' 91 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' 43 meters in diameter, 10.6 meters in height * '''Constriction Width:''' 24 meters * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' 55 meters long, 36 meters wide, 5 meters high
Similar to the Tsushimazuka Kofun, the front rectangular mound is notably longer compared to the rear circular mound, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl.
=== Burial Chamber === The burial facility consists of a horizontally-arranged stone chamber with a two-room structure: the main chamber (''gen-shitsu''), an antechamber (''zen-shitsu''), and a passage (''ensen-dō''), opening to the southwest. Records from the Edo period, such as ''Kōshi Yawa'' and ''Iki Meishō Zushi'', mention the already-opened chamber.
'''Chamber Dimensions:'''
* '''Total Length:''' 11 meters * '''Main Chamber:''' 3.5 meters long, 2.58 meters wide, 4.28 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 6.28 meters long, 1.8 meters wide, 1.68 meters high * '''Passage:''' 1.28 meters long, 1.28 meters wide, 1.7 meters high
Traces of red pigment are visible in the main chamber, and tuff paving stones cover the floor. Additionally, line engravings of boat shapes are found on the chamber walls.
=== Excavated Artifacts === Artifacts from the site include:
* '''Main Chamber:''' Sue pottery, Haji pottery, iron objects, glass beads, polychrome fragments, gold threads * '''Antechamber:''' Gilt-bronze sword hilts, pommel decorations, eight-window guards, ''unju'' ornaments, apricot-leaf pendants, gilt-bronze crowns, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, glass beads * '''Passage:''' Gilt-bronze bells, iron arrowheads, silver-inlaid guards, horse gear, Haji pottery, daggers * '''Entrance Area:''' Silla pottery
These artifacts, particularly the elaborately crafted horse gear and Silla imports, highlight advanced craftsmanship and international interactions during the period. They are collectively designated as ''Important Cultural Properties of Japan'' under the title "Excavated Artifacts from Sōroku Kofun."
=== Gallery ===
* Entrance of the stone chamber * Interior of the stone chamber
=== Cultural Designations ===
* '''Important Cultural Property (National Designation):''' ** ''Excavated Artifacts from Sōroku Kofun'' (designated in 2008), including: *** 8 gold items *** 54 silver items *** 58 gilt-bronze items *** 14 bronze items *** 102 iron items *** 93 glass items *** 10 stone items *** 1 amber bead *** 72 ceramic/earthenware items
=== Related Facilities ===
* '''Iki City Ikikoku Museum''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) – houses artifacts excavated from Sōroku Kofun.
=== External Links ===
* Iki Kofun Cluster - Nagasaki Prefectural Website

revision.comment: 6 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:双六古墳 up to rev 100827839
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 双六古墳 |画像 = [[File:Souroku Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション = 墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = [[壱岐古墳群]] |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]勝本町立石東触81 |緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 47|緯度秒 = 54.30 |経度度 = 129|経度分 = 42|経度秒 = 13.35 |ISO = JP-42 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長91m
高さ10.6m |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]]（2室） |出土品 = 金製品・銀製品・金銅製品ほか副葬品多数 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「壱岐古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = 出土品（国の[[重要文化財]]） |特記事項 = 長崎県第1位の規模 |地図 = Japan Nagasaki |アイコン = 前方後円墳-南南東 |ラベル = 双六古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''双六古墳'''（そうろくこふん）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]勝本町立石東触にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[壱岐古墳群]]（国の[[史跡]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。出土品は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 壱岐島、ひいては長崎県最大の規模の古墳で[http://www.ikikankou.com/wp/?p=3811 双六古墳]（壱岐市観光連盟「壱岐観光ナビ」）。、[[6世紀]]後半（[[古墳時代]]後期）頃の築造と推定される。 == 概要 == 壱岐島中央部の丘陵上に築造された古墳である。[[1981年]]（昭和56年）に松永泰彦による石室測量が、[[1992年]]（平成4年）に長崎県教育委員会による墳丘・石室の実測調査が、[[1997年]]-[[2000年]]（平成9-12年）に旧勝本町による本格的な発掘調査がなされている{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘は前方後円形で、2段築成である{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。規模は次の通り{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 墳丘長：91メートル * 後円部：直径43メートル、高さ10.6メートル * くびれ部：幅24メートル * 前方部：長さ55メートル、幅36メートル、高さ5メートル 対馬塚古墳同様、墳丘の前方部は後円部に比して長く、後円部はお椀を伏せたような急斜面をなす特徴を有している{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/239 双六古墳]（長崎県教育庁学芸文化課「長崎県の遺跡大辞典」）。。 == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設は複室構造の横穴式石室で、玄室・前室の2室と羨道で構成され、南西方に開口する{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。江戸時代にはすでに石室は開口し、『甲子夜話』『壱岐名勝図誌』に記述がある{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 石室全長：11メートル * 玄室：長さ3.5メートル、幅2.58メートル、高さ4.28メートル * 前室：長さ6.28メートル、幅1.8メートル、高さ1.68メートル * 羨道：長さ1.28メートル、幅1.28メートル、高さ1.7メートル 玄室には朱塗痕があるほか、玄室床面には全面に凝灰岩製の敷石が敷かれたと見られる{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。また、石室壁に船形の線刻画も見られる。 == 出土品 == 出土品としては、玄室からは須恵器・土師器・鉄製品・ガラス玉・二彩片・金糸などが、前室からは金銅製圭頭大刀把頭・把元・八窓鐔・雲珠・杏葉・金銅製冠・須恵器・土師器・ガラス玉などが、羨道からは金銅製鈴・鉄鏃・銀象嵌鐔・馬具・土師器・短刀などが、開口部からは新羅土器が見つかっている{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。これらのうち、馬具装飾には高い技術水準が指摘されるほか、大陸産・新羅産の遺物は当時の海外交渉を伝えるものとして重要視される。これらの遺物は「長崎県双六古墳出土品」として国の重要文化財に指定されている。 File:Souroku Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|開口部 File:Souroku Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|石室内部 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 長崎県双六古墳出土品（考古資料） - 明細は以下。2008年（平成20年）7月10日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|00011284|長崎県双六古墳出土品}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/481 長崎県双六古墳出土品]（長崎県ホームページ）。。 ** 金製品 8点 ** 銀製品 54点 ** 金銅製品 58点 ** 青銅製品 14点 ** 鉄製品 102点 ** ガラス製品 93点 ** 石製品 10点 ** 琥珀玉 1点 ** 陶器・土器 72点 == 関連施設 == * [[壱岐市立一支国博物館]]（壱岐市芦辺町深江鶴亀触） - 双六古墳の出土品を保管。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板 * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2017|chapter=|title=壱岐市の文化財|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|壱岐市の文化財|2017}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2001|chapter=双六古墳|title=長崎県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]43|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490433|ref={{Harvid|双六古墳（平凡社）|2001}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=田川肇|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/14748 |title=県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書 -県内古墳の墳丘・石室の資料化に伴う報告書-|series=長崎県文化財調査報告書第106集|publisher=長崎県教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=|title=双六古墳|series=壱岐市文化財調査報告書第7集|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=|title=壱岐の島の古墳群 -現状調査-|series=壱岐市文化財調査報告書第20集|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Soroku Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 壱岐古墳群] - 長崎県ホームページ「長崎県の文化財」 {{デフォルトソート:そうろくこふん}} [[Category:壱岐古墳群]] [[Category:長崎県の古墳]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:長崎県の重要文化財]] [[Category:長崎県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:装飾古墳]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 双六古墳 |画像 = [[File:Souroku Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション = 墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = [[壱岐古墳群]] |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]勝本町立石東触81 |緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 47|緯度秒 = 54.30 |経度度 = 129|経度分 = 42|経度秒 = 13.35 |ISO = JP-42 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長91m
高さ10.6m |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]]（2室） |出土品 = 金製品・銀製品・金銅製品ほか副葬品多数 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「壱岐古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = 出土品（国の[[重要文化財]]） |特記事項 = 長崎県第1位の規模 |地図 = Japan Nagasaki |アイコン = 前方後円墳-南南東 |ラベル = 双六古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''双六古墳'''（そうろくこふん）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]勝本町立石東触にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[壱岐古墳群]]（国の[[史跡]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。出土品は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 壱岐島、ひいては長崎県最大の規模の古墳で[http://www.ikikankou.com/wp/?p=3811 双六古墳]（壱岐市観光連盟「壱岐観光ナビ」）。、[[6世紀]]後半（[[古墳時代]]後期）頃の築造と推定される。 == 概要 == 壱岐島中央部の丘陵上に築造された古墳である。[[1981年]]（昭和56年）に松永泰彦による石室測量が、[[1992年]]（平成4年）に長崎県教育委員会による墳丘・石室の実測調査が、[[1997年]]-[[2000年]]（平成9-12年）に旧勝本町による本格的な発掘調査がなされている{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘は前方後円形で、2段築成である{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。規模は次の通り{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 墳丘長：91メートル * 後円部：直径43メートル、高さ10.6メートル * くびれ部：幅24メートル * 前方部：長さ55メートル、幅36メートル、高さ5メートル 対馬塚古墳同様、墳丘の前方部は後円部に比して長く、後円部はお椀を伏せたような急斜面をなす特徴を有している{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/239 双六古墳]（長崎県教育庁学芸文化課「長崎県の遺跡大辞典」）。。 == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設は複室構造の横穴式石室で、玄室・前室の2室と羨道で構成され、南西方に開口する{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。江戸時代にはすでに石室は開口し、『甲子夜話』『壱岐名勝図誌』に記述がある{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 石室全長：11メートル * 玄室：長さ3.5メートル、幅2.58メートル、高さ4.28メートル * 前室：長さ6.28メートル、幅1.8メートル、高さ1.68メートル * 羨道：長さ1.28メートル、幅1.28メートル、高さ1.7メートル 玄室には朱塗痕があるほか、玄室床面には全面に凝灰岩製の敷石が敷かれたと見られる{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。また、石室壁に船形の線刻画も見られる。 == 出土品 == 出土品としては、玄室からは須恵器・土師器・鉄製品・ガラス玉・二彩片・金糸などが、前室からは金銅製圭頭大刀把頭・把元・八窓鐔・雲珠・杏葉・金銅製冠・須恵器・土師器・ガラス玉などが、羨道からは金銅製鈴・鉄鏃・銀象嵌鐔・馬具・土師器・短刀などが、開口部からは新羅土器が見つかっている{{Sfn|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。これらのうち、馬具装飾には高い技術水準が指摘されるほか、大陸産・新羅産の遺物は当時の海外交渉を伝えるものとして重要視される。これらの遺物は「長崎県双六古墳出土品」として国の重要文化財に指定されている。 File:Souroku Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|開口部 File:Souroku Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|石室内部 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 長崎県双六古墳出土品（考古資料） - 明細は以下。2008年（平成20年）7月10日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|00011284|長崎県双六古墳出土品}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/481 長崎県双六古墳出土品]（長崎県ホームページ）。。 ** 金製品 8点 ** 銀製品 54点 ** 金銅製品 58点 ** 青銅製品 14点 ** 鉄製品 102点 ** ガラス製品 93点 ** 石製品 10点 ** 琥珀玉 1点 ** 陶器・土器 72点 == 関連施設 == * [[壱岐市立一支国博物館]]（壱岐市芦辺町深江鶴亀触） - 双六古墳の出土品を保管。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板 * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2017|chapter=|title=壱岐市の文化財|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|壱岐市の文化財|2017}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2001|chapter=双六古墳|title=長崎県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]43|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490433|ref={{Harvid|双六古墳（平凡社）|2001}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=田川肇|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|双六古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/14748 |title=県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書 -県内古墳の墳丘・石室の資料化に伴う報告書-|series=長崎県文化財調査報告書第106集|publisher=長崎県教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=|title=双六古墳|series=壱岐市文化財調査報告書第7集|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=|title=壱岐の島の古墳群 -現状調査-|series=壱岐市文化財調査報告書第20集|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Soroku Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 壱岐古墳群] - 長崎県ホームページ「長崎県の文化財」 {{デフォルトソート:そうろくこふん}} [[Category:壱岐古墳群]] [[Category:長崎県の古墳]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:長崎県の重要文化財]] [[Category:長崎県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:装飾古墳]]

'''Sōroku Kofun'''
'''Sōroku Kofun''' (双六古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') located in Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is part of the Iki Kofun Cluster, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan. Artifacts excavated from the site are designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.
This kofun is the largest in Iki Island and Nagasaki Prefecture, estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century during the Kofun period.
== Overview == The kofun is situated on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Stone chamber surveys were conducted in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, followed by mound and chamber surveys in 1992 by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education. Extensive excavations were carried out by the former Katsumoto Town from 1997 to 2000.
=== Mound === The mound is keyhole-shaped with a two-tiered structure. The dimensions are as follows:
* '''Total Length:''' 91 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' 43 meters in diameter, 10.6 meters in height * '''Constriction Width:''' 24 meters * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' 55 meters long, 36 meters wide, 5 meters high
Similar to the Tsushimazuka Kofun, the front rectangular mound is notably longer compared to the rear circular mound, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl.
=== Burial Chamber === The burial facility consists of a horizontally-arranged stone chamber with a two-room structure: the main chamber (''gen-shitsu''), an antechamber (''zen-shitsu''), and a passage (''ensen-dō''), opening to the southwest. Records from the Edo period, such as ''Kōshi Yawa'' and ''Iki Meishō Zushi'', mention the already-opened chamber.
'''Chamber Dimensions:'''
* '''Total Length:''' 11 meters * '''Main Chamber:''' 3.5 meters long, 2.58 meters wide, 4.28 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 6.28 meters long, 1.8 meters wide, 1.68 meters high * '''Passage:''' 1.28 meters long, 1.28 meters wide, 1.7 meters high
Traces of red pigment are visible in the main chamber, and tuff paving stones cover the floor. Additionally, line engravings of boat shapes are found on the chamber walls.
=== Excavated Artifacts === Artifacts from the site include:
* '''Main Chamber:''' Sue pottery, Haji pottery, iron objects, glass beads, polychrome fragments, gold threads * '''Antechamber:''' Gilt-bronze sword hilts, pommel decorations, eight-window guards, ''unju'' ornaments, apricot-leaf pendants, gilt-bronze crowns, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, glass beads * '''Passage:''' Gilt-bronze bells, iron arrowheads, silver-inlaid guards, horse gear, Haji pottery, daggers * '''Entrance Area:''' Silla pottery
These artifacts, particularly the elaborately crafted horse gear and Silla imports, highlight advanced craftsmanship and international interactions during the period. They are collectively designated as ''Important Cultural Properties of Japan'' under the title "Excavated Artifacts from Sōroku Kofun."
=== Gallery ===
* Entrance of the stone chamber * Interior of the stone chamber
=== Cultural Designations ===
* '''Important Cultural Property (National Designation):''' ** ''Excavated Artifacts from Sōroku Kofun'' (designated in 2008), including: *** 8 gold items *** 54 silver items *** 58 gilt-bronze items *** 14 bronze items *** 102 iron items *** 93 glass items *** 10 stone items *** 1 amber bead *** 72 ceramic/earthenware items
=== Related Facilities ===
* '''Iki City Ikikoku Museum''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) – houses artifacts excavated from Sōroku Kofun.
=== External Links ===
* Iki Kofun Cluster - Nagasaki Prefectural Website

[[sn:Sōroku Kofun]] [[en:Sugoroku Kofun]] [[ja:双六古墳]]

'''Sōroku Kofun'''
'''Sōroku Kofun''' (双六古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') located in Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is part of the Iki Kofun Cluster, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan. Artifacts excavated from the site are designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.
This kofun is the largest in Iki Island and Nagasaki Prefecture, estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century during the Kofun period.
== Overview == The kofun is situated on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Stone chamber surveys were conducted in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, followed by mound and chamber surveys in 1992 by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education. Extensive excavations were carried out by the former Katsumoto Town from 1997 to 2000.
=== Mound === The mound is keyhole-shaped with a two-tiered structure. The dimensions are as follows:
* '''Total Length:''' 91 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' 43 meters in diameter, 10.6 meters in height * '''Constriction Width:''' 24 meters * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' 55 meters long, 36 meters wide, 5 meters high
Similar to the Tsushimazuka Kofun, the front rectangular mound is notably longer compared to the rear circular mound, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl.
=== Burial Chamber === The burial facility consists of a horizontally-arranged stone chamber with a two-room structure: the main chamber (''gen-shitsu''), an antechamber (''zen-shitsu''), and a passage (''ensen-dō''), opening to the southwest. Records from the Edo period, such as ''Kōshi Yawa'' and ''Iki Meishō Zushi'', mention the already-opened chamber.
'''Chamber Dimensions:'''
* '''Total Length:''' 11 meters * '''Main Chamber:''' 3.5 meters long, 2.58 meters wide, 4.28 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 6.28 meters long, 1.8 meters wide, 1.68 meters high * '''Passage:''' 1.28 meters long, 1.28 meters wide, 1.7 meters high
Traces of red pigment are visible in the main chamber, and tuff paving stones cover the floor. Additionally, line engravings of boat shapes are found on the chamber walls.
=== Excavated Artifacts === Artifacts from the site include:
* '''Main Chamber:''' Sue pottery, Haji pottery, iron objects, glass beads, polychrome fragments, gold threads * '''Antechamber:''' Gilt-bronze sword hilts, pommel decorations, eight-window guards, ''unju'' ornaments, apricot-leaf pendants, gilt-bronze crowns, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, glass beads * '''Passage:''' Gilt-bronze bells, iron arrowheads, silver-inlaid guards, horse gear, Haji pottery, daggers * '''Entrance Area:''' Silla pottery
These artifacts, particularly the elaborately crafted horse gear and Silla imports, highlight advanced craftsmanship and international interactions during the period. They are collectively designated as ''Important Cultural Properties of Japan'' under the title "Excavated Artifacts from Sōroku Kofun."
=== Gallery ===
* Entrance of the stone chamber * Interior of the stone chamber
=== Cultural Designations ===
* '''Important Cultural Property (National Designation):''' ** ''Excavated Artifacts from Sōroku Kofun'' (designated in 2008), including: *** 8 gold items *** 54 silver items *** 58 gilt-bronze items *** 14 bronze items *** 102 iron items *** 93 glass items *** 10 stone items *** 1 amber bead *** 72 ceramic/earthenware items
=== Related Facilities ===
* '''Iki City Ikikoku Museum''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) – houses artifacts excavated from Sōroku Kofun.
=== External Links ===
* Iki Kofun Cluster - Nagasaki Prefectural Website

[[sn:Sōroku Kofun]] [[en:Sugoroku Kofun]] [[ja:双六古墳]]

'''Sōroku Kofun'''
'''Sōroku Kofun''' (双六古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') located in Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is part of the Iki Kofun Cluster, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan. Artifacts excavated from the site are designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.
This kofun is the largest in Iki Island and Nagasaki Prefecture, estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century during the Kofun period.
== Overview == The kofun is situated on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Stone chamber surveys were conducted in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, followed by mound and chamber surveys in 1992 by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education. Extensive excavations were carried out by the former Katsumoto Town from 1997 to 2000.
=== Mound === The mound is keyhole-shaped with a two-tiered structure. The dimensions are as follows:
* '''Total Length:''' 91 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' 43 meters in diameter, 10.6 meters in height * '''Constriction Width:''' 24 meters * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' 55 meters long, 36 meters wide, 5 meters high
Similar to the Tsushimazuka Kofun, the front rectangular mound is notably longer compared to the rear circular mound, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl.
=== Burial Chamber === The burial facility consists of a horizontally-arranged stone chamber with a two-room structure: the main chamber (''gen-shitsu''), an antechamber (''zen-shitsu''), and a passage (''ensen-dō''), opening to the southwest. Records from the Edo period, such as ''Kōshi Yawa'' and ''Iki Meishō Zushi'', mention the already-opened chamber.
'''Chamber Dimensions:'''
* '''Total Length:''' 11 meters * '''Main Chamber:''' 3.5 meters long, 2.58 meters wide, 4.28 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 6.28 meters long, 1.8 meters wide, 1.68 meters high * '''Passage:''' 1.28 meters long, 1.28 meters wide, 1.7 meters high
Traces of red pigment are visible in the main chamber, and tuff paving stones cover the floor. Additionally, line engravings of boat shapes are found on the chamber walls.
=== Excavated Artifacts === Artifacts from the site include:
* '''Main Chamber:''' Sue pottery, Haji pottery, iron objects, glass beads, polychrome fragments, gold threads * '''Antechamber:''' Gilt-bronze sword hilts, pommel decorations, eight-window guards, ''unju'' ornaments, apricot-leaf pendants, gilt-bronze crowns, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, glass beads * '''Passage:''' Gilt-bronze bells, iron arrowheads, silver-inlaid guards, horse gear, Haji pottery, daggers * '''Entrance Area:''' Silla pottery
These artifacts, particularly the elaborately crafted horse gear and Silla imports, highlight advanced craftsmanship and international interactions during the period. They are collectively designated as ''Important Cultural Properties of Japan'' under the title "Excavated Artifacts from Sōroku Kofun."
=== Gallery ===
* Entrance of the stone chamber * Interior of the stone chamber
=== Cultural Designations ===
* '''Important Cultural Property (National Designation):''' ** ''Excavated Artifacts from Sōroku Kofun'' (designated in 2008), including: *** 8 gold items *** 54 silver items *** 58 gilt-bronze items *** 14 bronze items *** 102 iron items *** 93 glass items *** 10 stone items *** 1 amber bead *** 72 ceramic/earthenware items
=== Related Facilities ===
* '''Iki City Ikikoku Museum''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) – houses artifacts excavated from Sōroku Kofun.
=== External Links ===
* Iki Kofun Cluster - Nagasaki Prefectural Website
----'''Categories:'''
* Iki Kofun Cluster * Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture * History of Iki City * Keyhole-shaped Kofun * Important Cultural Properties of Japan (Nagasaki Prefecture) * National Historic Sites of Japan (Nagasaki Prefecture) * Decorated Kofun
* * [[Iki Kofun Cluster|Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|lt=Sōroku Kofun|lt=Sugoroku Kofun|ja|'''双六古墳'''|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugoroku Kofun|qq=}} - Keyhole-shaped mound (total length: 91 meters; the largest in Iki Islan ...{{ill|Tsushima-tsuka Kofun|ja|対馬塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima-tsuka Kofun|qq=}}, followed by {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|lt=Sōroku Kofun|lt=Sugoroku Kofun|ja|'''双六古墳'''|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugoroku Kofun|qq=}}, and later transitioned to circular mounds by the end of the century. * '''Total Length:''' 91 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' 43 meters in diameter, 10.6 meters in height * '''Constriction Width:''' 24 meters * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' 55 meters long, 36 meters wide, 5 meters high
Similar to the Tsushimazuka Kofun, the front rectangular mound is notably longer compared to the rear circular mound, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl.

Records from the Edo period, such as ''Kōshi Yawa'' and ''Iki Meishō Zushi'', mention the already-opened chamber.
'''Chamber Dimensions:'''
* '''Total Length:''' 11 meters * '''Main Chamber:''' 3.5 meters long, 2.58 meters wide, 4.28 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 6.28 meters long, 1.8 meters wide, 1.68 meters high * '''Passage:''' 1.28 meters long, 1.28 meters wide, 1.7 meters high
Traces of red pigment are visible in the main chamber, and tuff paving stones cover the floor. Additionally, line engravings of boat shapes are found on the chamber walls.
=== Excavated Artifacts === Artifacts from the site include:
* '''Main Chamber:''' Sue pottery, Haji pottery, iron objects, glass beads, polychrome fragments, gold threads * '''Antechamber:''' Gilt-bronze sword hilts, pommel decorations, eight-window guards, ''unju'' ornaments, apricot-leaf pendants, gilt-bronze crowns, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, glass beads * '''Passage:''' Gilt-bronze bells, iron arrowheads, silver-inlaid guards, horse gear, Haji pottery, daggers * '''Entrance Area:''' Silla pottery
These artifacts, particularly the elaborately crafted horse gear and Silla imports, highlight advanced craftsmanship and international interactions during the period. They are collectively designated as ''Important Cultural Properties of Japan'' under the title "Excavated Artifacts from Sōroku Kofun."

revision.comment: Bot: update category → Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nagasaki prefecture
revision.text:
'''Sōroku Kofun'''
'''Sōroku Kofun''' (双六古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') located in Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is part of the Iki Kofun Cluster, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan. Artifacts excavated from the site are designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.
This kofun is the largest in Iki Island and Nagasaki Prefecture, estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century during the Kofun period.
== Overview == The kofun is situated on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Stone chamber surveys were conducted in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, followed by mound and chamber surveys in 1992 by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education. Extensive excavations were carried out by the former Katsumoto Town from 1997 to 2000.
=== Mound === The mound is keyhole-shaped with a two-tiered structure. The dimensions are as follows:
* '''Total Length:''' 91 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' 43 meters in diameter, 10.6 meters in height * '''Constriction Width:''' 24 meters * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' 55 meters long, 36 meters wide, 5 meters high
Similar to the Tsushimazuka Kofun, the front rectangular mound is notably longer compared to the rear circular mound, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl.
=== Burial Chamber === The burial facility consists of a horizontally-arranged stone chamber with a two-room structure: the main chamber (''gen-shitsu''), an antechamber (''zen-shitsu''), and a passage (''ensen-dō''), opening to the southwest. Records from the Edo period, such as ''Kōshi Yawa'' and ''Iki Meishō Zushi'', mention the already-opened chamber.
'''Chamber Dimensions:'''
* '''Total Length:''' 11 meters * '''Main Chamber:''' 3.5 meters long, 2.58 meters wide, 4.28 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 6.28 meters long, 1.8 meters wide, 1.68 meters high * '''Passage:''' 1.28 meters long, 1.28 meters wide, 1.7 meters high
Traces of red pigment are visible in the main chamber, and tuff paving stones cover the floor. Additionally, line engravings of boat shapes are found on the chamber walls.
=== Excavated Artifacts === Artifacts from the site include:
* '''Main Chamber:''' Sue pottery, Haji pottery, iron objects, glass beads, polychrome fragments, gold threads * '''Antechamber:''' Gilt-bronze sword hilts, pommel decorations, eight-window guards, ''unju'' ornaments, apricot-leaf pendants, gilt-bronze crowns, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, glass beads * '''Passage:''' Gilt-bronze bells, iron arrowheads, silver-inlaid guards, horse gear, Haji pottery, daggers * '''Entrance Area:''' Silla pottery
These artifacts, particularly the elaborately crafted horse gear and Silla imports, highlight advanced craftsmanship and international interactions during the period. They are collectively designated as ''Important Cultural Properties of Japan'' under the title "Excavated Artifacts from Sōroku Kofun."
=== Gallery ===
* Entrance of the stone chamber * Interior of the stone chamber
=== Cultural Designations ===
* '''Important Cultural Property (National Designation):''' ** ''Excavated Artifacts from Sōroku Kofun'' (designated in 2008), including: *** 8 gold items *** 54 silver items *** 58 gilt-bronze items *** 14 bronze items *** 102 iron items *** 93 glass items *** 10 stone items *** 1 amber bead *** 72 ceramic/earthenware items
=== Related Facilities ===
* '''Iki City Ikikoku Museum''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) – houses artifacts excavated from Sōroku Kofun.
=== External Links ===
* Iki Kofun Cluster - Nagasaki Prefectural Website
----'''Categories:'''
* Iki Kofun Cluster * Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture * History of Iki City * Keyhole-shaped Kofun * Important Cultural Properties of Japan (Nagasaki Prefecture) * National Historic Sites of Japan (Nagasaki Prefecture) * Decorated Kofun
* * [[Iki Kofun Cluster|Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|lt=Sōroku Kofun|lt=Sugoroku Kofun|ja|'''双六古墳'''|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugoroku Kofun|qq=}} - Keyhole-shaped mound (total length: 91 meters; the largest in Iki Islan ...{{ill|Tsushima-tsuka Kofun|ja|対馬塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima-tsuka Kofun|qq=}}, followed by {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|lt=Sōroku Kofun|lt=Sugoroku Kofun|ja|'''双六古墳'''|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugoroku Kofun|qq=}}, and later transitioned to circular mounds by the end of the century. [[ja:双六古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Iki Kofun Group�]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:Decorated kofun]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nagasaki prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Soroku Kofun}}

'''Sōroku Kofun'''
'''Sōroku Kofun''' (双六古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') located in Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is part of the Iki Kofun Cluster, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan. Artifacts excavated from the site are designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.
This kofun is the largest in Iki Island and Nagasaki Prefecture, estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century during the Kofun period.
== Overview == The kofun is situated on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Stone chamber surveys were conducted in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, followed by mound and chamber surveys in 1992 by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education. Extensive excavations were carried out by the former Katsumoto Town from 1997 to 2000.
=== Mound === The mound is keyhole-shaped with a two-tiered structure. The dimensions are as follows:
* '''Total Length:''' 91 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' 43 meters in diameter, 10.6 meters in height * '''Constriction Width:''' 24 meters * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' 55 meters long, 36 meters wide, 5 meters high
Similar to the Tsushimazuka Kofun, the front rectangular mound is notably longer compared to the rear circular mound, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl.
=== Burial Chamber === The burial facility consists of a horizontally-arranged stone chamber with a two-room structure: the main chamber (''gen-shitsu''), an antechamber (''zen-shitsu''), and a passage (''ensen-dō''), opening to the southwest. Records from the Edo period, such as ''Kōshi Yawa'' and ''Iki Meishō Zushi'', mention the already-opened chamber.
'''Chamber Dimensions:'''
* '''Total Length:''' 11 meters * '''Main Chamber:''' 3.5 meters long, 2.58 meters wide, 4.28 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 6.28 meters long, 1.8 meters wide, 1.68 meters high * '''Passage:''' 1.28 meters long, 1.28 meters wide, 1.7 meters high
Traces of red pigment are visible in the main chamber, and tuff paving stones cover the floor. Additionally, line engravings of boat shapes are found on the chamber walls.
=== Excavated Artifacts === Artifacts from the site include:
* '''Main Chamber:''' Sue pottery, Haji pottery, iron objects, glass beads, polychrome fragments, gold threads * '''Antechamber:''' Gilt-bronze sword hilts, pommel decorations, eight-window guards, ''unju'' ornaments, apricot-leaf pendants, gilt-bronze crowns, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, glass beads * '''Passage:''' Gilt-bronze bells, iron arrowheads, silver-inlaid guards, horse gear, Haji pottery, daggers * '''Entrance Area:''' Silla pottery
These artifacts, particularly the elaborately crafted horse gear and Silla imports, highlight advanced craftsmanship and international interactions during the period. They are collectively designated as ''Important Cultural Properties of Japan'' under the title "Excavated Artifacts from Sōroku Kofun."
=== Gallery ===
* Entrance of the stone chamber * Interior of the stone chamber
=== Cultural Designations ===
* '''Important Cultural Property (National Designation):''' ** ''Excavated Artifacts from Sōroku Kofun'' (designated in 2008), including: *** 8 gold items *** 54 silver items *** 58 gilt-bronze items *** 14 bronze items *** 102 iron items *** 93 glass items *** 10 stone items *** 1 amber bead *** 72 ceramic/earthenware items
=== Related Facilities ===
* '''Iki City Ikikoku Museum''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) – houses artifacts excavated from Sōroku Kofun.
=== External Links ===
* Iki Kofun Cluster - Nagasaki Prefectural Website

These artifacts, particularly the elaborately crafted horse gear and Silla imports, highlight advanced craftsmanship and international interactions during the period. {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q126605027]] Wikidata name: Soroku Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sōroku Kofun'''
'''Sōroku Kofun''' (双六古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') located in Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is part of the Iki Kofun Cluster, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan. Artifacts excavated from the site are designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.
This kofun is the largest in Iki Island and Nagasaki Prefecture, estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century during the Kofun period.
== Overview == The kofun is situated on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Stone chamber surveys were conducted in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, followed by mound and chamber surveys in 1992 by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education. Extensive excavations were carried out by the former Katsumoto Town from 1997 to 2000.
=== Mound === The mound is keyhole-shaped with a two-tiered structure. The dimensions are as follows:
* '''Total Length:''' 91 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' 43 meters in diameter, 10.6 meters in height * '''Constriction Width:''' 24 meters * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' 55 meters long, 36 meters wide, 5 meters high
Similar to the Tsushimazuka Kofun, the front rectangular mound is notably longer compared to the rear circular mound, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl.
=== Burial Chamber === The burial facility consists of a horizontally-arranged stone chamber with a two-room structure: the main chamber (''gen-shitsu''), an antechamber (''zen-shitsu''), and a passage (''ensen-dō''), opening to the southwest. Records from the Edo period, such as ''Kōshi Yawa'' and ''Iki Meishō Zushi'', mention the already-opened chamber.
'''Chamber Dimensions:'''
* '''Total Length:''' 11 meters * '''Main Chamber:''' 3.5 meters long, 2.58 meters wide, 4.28 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 6.28 meters long, 1.8 meters wide, 1.68 meters high * '''Passage:''' 1.28 meters long, 1.28 meters wide, 1.7 meters high
Traces of red pigment are visible in the main chamber, and tuff paving stones cover the floor. Additionally, line engravings of boat shapes are found on the chamber walls.
=== Excavated Artifacts === Artifacts from the site include:
* '''Main Chamber:''' Sue pottery, Haji pottery, iron objects, glass beads, polychrome fragments, gold threads * '''Antechamber:''' Gilt-bronze sword hilts, pommel decorations, eight-window guards, ''unju'' ornaments, apricot-leaf pendants, gilt-bronze crowns, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, glass beads * '''Passage:''' Gilt-bronze bells, iron arrowheads, silver-inlaid guards, horse gear, Haji pottery, daggers * '''Entrance Area:''' Silla pottery
These artifacts, particularly the elaborately crafted horse gear and Silla imports, highlight advanced craftsmanship and international interactions during the period. They are collectively designated as ''Important Cultural Properties of Japan'' under the title "Excavated Artifacts from Sōroku Kofun."
=== Gallery ===
* Entrance of the stone chamber * Interior of the stone chamber
=== Cultural Designations ===
* '''Important Cultural Property (National Designation):''' ** ''Excavated Artifacts from Sōroku Kofun'' (designated in 2008), including: *** 8 gold items *** 54 silver items *** 58 gilt-bronze items *** 14 bronze items *** 102 iron items *** 93 glass items *** 10 stone items *** 1 amber bead *** 72 ceramic/earthenware items
=== Related Facilities ===
* '''Iki City Ikikoku Museum''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) – houses artifacts excavated from Sōroku Kofun.

[[ja:双六古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Iki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Decorated kofun]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nagasaki prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Soroku Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q126605027}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Soroku Kofun]][[ja:双六古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q126605027]] Wikidata name: Soroku Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sōroku Kofun'''
'''Sōroku Kofun''' (双六古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') located in Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is part of the Iki Kofun Cluster, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan. Artifacts excavated from the site are designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.
This kofun is the largest in Iki Island and Nagasaki Prefecture, estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century during the Kofun period.
== Overview == The kofun is situated on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Stone chamber surveys were conducted in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, followed by mound and chamber surveys in 1992 by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education. Extensive excavations were carried out by the former Katsumoto Town from 1997 to 2000.
=== Mound === The mound is keyhole-shaped with a two-tiered structure. The dimensions are as follows:
* '''Total Length:''' 91 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' 43 meters in diameter, 10.6 meters in height * '''Constriction Width:''' 24 meters * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' 55 meters long, 36 meters wide, 5 meters high
Similar to the Tsushimazuka Kofun, the front rectangular mound is notably longer compared to the rear circular mound, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl.
=== Burial Chamber === The burial facility consists of a horizontally-arranged stone chamber with a two-room structure: the main chamber (''gen-shitsu''), an antechamber (''zen-shitsu''), and a passage (''ensen-dō''), opening to the southwest. Records from the Edo period, such as ''Kōshi Yawa'' and ''Iki Meishō Zushi'', mention the already-opened chamber.
'''Chamber Dimensions:'''
* '''Total Length:''' 11 meters * '''Main Chamber:''' 3.5 meters long, 2.58 meters wide, 4.28 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 6.28 meters long, 1.8 meters wide, 1.68 meters high * '''Passage:''' 1.28 meters long, 1.28 meters wide, 1.7 meters high
Traces of red pigment are visible in the main chamber, and tuff paving stones cover the floor. Additionally, line engravings of boat shapes are found on the chamber walls.
=== Excavated Artifacts === Artifacts from the site include:
* '''Main Chamber:''' Sue pottery, Haji pottery, iron objects, glass beads, polychrome fragments, gold threads * '''Antechamber:''' Gilt-bronze sword hilts, pommel decorations, eight-window guards, ''unju'' ornaments, apricot-leaf pendants, gilt-bronze crowns, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, glass beads * '''Passage:''' Gilt-bronze bells, iron arrowheads, silver-inlaid guards, horse gear, Haji pottery, daggers * '''Entrance Area:''' Silla pottery
These artifacts, particularly the elaborately crafted horse gear and Silla imports, highlight advanced craftsmanship and international interactions during the period. They are collectively designated as ''Important Cultural Properties of Japan'' under the title "Excavated Artifacts from Sōroku Kofun."
=== Gallery ===
* Entrance of the stone chamber * Interior of the stone chamber
=== Cultural Designations ===
* '''Important Cultural Property (National Designation):''' ** ''Excavated Artifacts from Sōroku Kofun'' (designated in 2008), including: *** 8 gold items *** 54 silver items *** 58 gilt-bronze items *** 14 bronze items *** 102 iron items *** 93 glass items *** 10 stone items *** 1 amber bead *** 72 ceramic/earthenware items
=== Related Facilities ===
* '''Iki City Ikikoku Museum''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) – houses artifacts excavated from Sōroku Kofun.

[[ja:双六古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Iki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Decorated kofun]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nagasaki prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Soroku Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q126605027}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Soroku Kofun]][[ja:双六古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q126605027]] Wikidata name: Soroku Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sōroku Kofun'''
'''Sōroku Kofun''' (双六古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') located in Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is part of the Iki Kofun Cluster, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan. Artifacts excavated from the site are designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.
This kofun is the largest in Iki Island and Nagasaki Prefecture, estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century during the Kofun period.
== Overview == The kofun is situated on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Stone chamber surveys were conducted in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, followed by mound and chamber surveys in 1992 by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education. Extensive excavations were carried out by the former Katsumoto Town from 1997 to 2000.
=== Mound === The mound is keyhole-shaped with a two-tiered structure. The dimensions are as follows:
* '''Total Length:''' 91 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' 43 meters in diameter, 10.6 meters in height * '''Constriction Width:''' 24 meters * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' 55 meters long, 36 meters wide, 5 meters high
Similar to the Tsushimazuka Kofun, the front rectangular mound is notably longer compared to the rear circular mound, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl.
=== Burial Chamber === The burial facility consists of a horizontally-arranged stone chamber with a two-room structure: the main chamber (''gen-shitsu''), an antechamber (''zen-shitsu''), and a passage (''ensen-dō''), opening to the southwest. Records from the Edo period, such as ''Kōshi Yawa'' and ''Iki Meishō Zushi'', mention the already-opened chamber.
'''Chamber Dimensions:'''
* '''Total Length:''' 11 meters * '''Main Chamber:''' 3.5 meters long, 2.58 meters wide, 4.28 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 6.28 meters long, 1.8 meters wide, 1.68 meters high * '''Passage:''' 1.28 meters long, 1.28 meters wide, 1.7 meters high
Traces of red pigment are visible in the main chamber, and tuff paving stones cover the floor. Additionally, line engravings of boat shapes are found on the chamber walls.
=== Excavated Artifacts === Artifacts from the site include:
* '''Main Chamber:''' Sue pottery, Haji pottery, iron objects, glass beads, polychrome fragments, gold threads * '''Antechamber:''' Gilt-bronze sword hilts, pommel decorations, eight-window guards, ''unju'' ornaments, apricot-leaf pendants, gilt-bronze crowns, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, glass beads * '''Passage:''' Gilt-bronze bells, iron arrowheads, silver-inlaid guards, horse gear, Haji pottery, daggers * '''Entrance Area:''' Silla pottery
These artifacts, particularly the elaborately crafted horse gear and Silla imports, highlight advanced craftsmanship and international interactions during the period. They are collectively designated as ''Important Cultural Properties of Japan'' under the title "Excavated Artifacts from Sōroku Kofun."
=== Gallery ===
* Entrance of the stone chamber * Interior of the stone chamber
=== Cultural Designations ===
* '''Important Cultural Property (National Designation):''' ** ''Excavated Artifacts from Sōroku Kofun'' (designated in 2008), including: *** 8 gold items *** 54 silver items *** 58 gilt-bronze items *** 14 bronze items *** 102 iron items *** 93 glass items *** 10 stone items *** 1 amber bead *** 72 ceramic/earthenware items
=== Related Facilities ===
* '''Iki City Ikikoku Museum''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) – houses artifacts excavated from Sōroku Kofun.
=== External Links ===
* Iki Kofun Cluster - Nagasaki Prefectural Website

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Soroku Kofun]][[ja:双六古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q126605027]] Wikidata name: Soroku Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sōroku Kofun'''
'''Sōroku Kofun''' (双六古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') located in Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is part of the Iki Kofun Cluster, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan. Artifacts excavated from the site are designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.
This kofun is the largest in Iki Island and Nagasaki Prefecture, estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century during the Kofun period.
== Overview == The kofun is situated on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Stone chamber surveys were conducted in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, followed by mound and chamber surveys in 1992 by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education. Extensive excavations were carried out by the former Katsumoto Town from 1997 to 2000.
=== Mound === The mound is keyhole-shaped with a two-tiered structure. The dimensions are as follows:
* '''Total Length:''' 91 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' 43 meters in diameter, 10.6 meters in height * '''Constriction Width:''' 24 meters * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' 55 meters long, 36 meters wide, 5 meters high
Similar to the Tsushimazuka Kofun, the front rectangular mound is notably longer compared to the rear circular mound, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl.
=== Burial Chamber === The burial facility consists of a horizontally-arranged stone chamber with a two-room structure: the main chamber (''gen-shitsu''), an antechamber (''zen-shitsu''), and a passage (''ensen-dō''), opening to the southwest. Records from the Edo period, such as ''Kōshi Yawa'' and ''Iki Meishō Zushi'', mention the already-opened chamber.
'''Chamber Dimensions:'''
* '''Total Length:''' 11 meters * '''Main Chamber:''' 3.5 meters long, 2.58 meters wide, 4.28 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 6.28 meters long, 1.8 meters wide, 1.68 meters high * '''Passage:''' 1.28 meters long, 1.28 meters wide, 1.7 meters high
Traces of red pigment are visible in the main chamber, and tuff paving stones cover the floor. Additionally, line engravings of boat shapes are found on the chamber walls.
=== Excavated Artifacts === Artifacts from the site include:
* '''Main Chamber:''' Sue pottery, Haji pottery, iron objects, glass beads, polychrome fragments, gold threads * '''Antechamber:''' Gilt-bronze sword hilts, pommel decorations, eight-window guards, ''unju'' ornaments, apricot-leaf pendants, gilt-bronze crowns, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, glass beads * '''Passage:''' Gilt-bronze bells, iron arrowheads, silver-inlaid guards, horse gear, Haji pottery, daggers * '''Entrance Area:''' Silla pottery
These artifacts, particularly the elaborately crafted horse gear and Silla imports, highlight advanced craftsmanship and international interactions during the period. They are collectively designated as ''Important Cultural Properties of Japan'' under the title "Excavated Artifacts from Sōroku Kofun."
=== Gallery ===
* Entrance of the stone chamber * Interior of the stone chamber
=== Cultural Designations ===
* '''Important Cultural Property (National Designation):''' ** ''Excavated Artifacts from Sōroku Kofun'' (designated in 2008), including: *** 8 gold items *** 54 silver items *** 58 gilt-bronze items *** 14 bronze items *** 102 iron items *** 93 glass items *** 10 stone items *** 1 amber bead *** 72 ceramic/earthenware items
=== Related Facilities ===
* '''Iki City Ikikoku Museum''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) – houses artifacts excavated from Sōroku Kofun.
=== External Links ===
* Iki Kofun Cluster - Nagasaki Prefectural Website

[[ja:双六古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Iki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Decorated kofun]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nagasaki prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Soroku Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q126605027}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Soroku Kofun]][[ja:双六古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q126605027]] Wikidata name: Soroku Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sōroku Kofun'''
'''Sōroku Kofun''' (双六古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') located in Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is part of the Iki Kofun Cluster, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan. Artifacts excavated from the site are designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.
This kofun is the largest in Iki Island and Nagasaki Prefecture, estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century during the Kofun period.
== Overview == The kofun is situated on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Stone chamber surveys were conducted in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, followed by mound and chamber surveys in 1992 by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education. Extensive excavations were carried out by the former Katsumoto Town from 1997 to 2000.
=== Mound === The mound is keyhole-shaped with a two-tiered structure. The dimensions are as follows:
* '''Total Length:''' 91 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' 43 meters in diameter, 10.6 meters in height * '''Constriction Width:''' 24 meters * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' 55 meters long, 36 meters wide, 5 meters high
Similar to the Tsushimazuka Kofun, the front rectangular mound is notably longer compared to the rear circular mound, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl.
=== Burial Chamber === The burial facility consists of a horizontally-arranged stone chamber with a two-room structure: the main chamber (''gen-shitsu''), an antechamber (''zen-shitsu''), and a passage (''ensen-dō''), opening to the southwest. Records from the Edo period, such as ''Kōshi Yawa'' and ''Iki Meishō Zushi'', mention the already-opened chamber.
'''Chamber Dimensions:'''
* '''Total Length:''' 11 meters * '''Main Chamber:''' 3.5 meters long, 2.58 meters wide, 4.28 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 6.28 meters long, 1.8 meters wide, 1.68 meters high * '''Passage:''' 1.28 meters long, 1.28 meters wide, 1.7 meters high
Traces of red pigment are visible in the main chamber, and tuff paving stones cover the floor. Additionally, line engravings of boat shapes are found on the chamber walls.
=== Excavated Artifacts === Artifacts from the site include:
* '''Main Chamber:''' Sue pottery, Haji pottery, iron objects, glass beads, polychrome fragments, gold threads * '''Antechamber:''' Gilt-bronze sword hilts, pommel decorations, eight-window guards, ''unju'' ornaments, apricot-leaf pendants, gilt-bronze crowns, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, glass beads * '''Passage:''' Gilt-bronze bells, iron arrowheads, silver-inlaid guards, horse gear, Haji pottery, daggers * '''Entrance Area:''' Silla pottery
These artifacts, particularly the elaborately crafted horse gear and Silla imports, highlight advanced craftsmanship and international interactions during the period. They are collectively designated as ''Important Cultural Properties of Japan'' under the title "Excavated Artifacts from Sōroku Kofun."
=== Gallery ===
* Entrance of the stone chamber * Interior of the stone chamber
=== Cultural Designations ===
* '''Important Cultural Property (National Designation):''' ** ''Excavated Artifacts from Sōroku Kofun'' (designated in 2008), including: *** 8 gold items *** 54 silver items *** 58 gilt-bronze items *** 14 bronze items *** 102 iron items *** 93 glass items *** 10 stone items *** 1 amber bead *** 72 ceramic/earthenware items
=== Related Facilities ===
* '''Iki City Ikikoku Museum''' (Ashibe Town, Iki City) – houses artifacts excavated from Sōroku Kofun.
=== External Links ===
* Iki Kofun Cluster - Nagasaki Prefectural Website
